ety: v.n. fr. مکن 'to be firmly fixed,' &c.
def: adj. Firmly fixed; well-established; — in a high station; authoritative; — grave; — s.m. & f. Inhabitant, dweller; — master, or mistress, of a house.
s.f. A hen, fowl.
s.m. Indian corn, maize, Zea mays.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀕 (*makkaga), from Sanskrit मर्कक (markaka, “Ardea argala”).

def: (for A. مکة makkat), prop. n. The city of Mecca, in Arabia (the birthplace of Moḥammad).
s.m. A large spider; a spider; — a tall, lean person; — a kind of grain (cf. makkā)
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Cobwebbed; formed like a cobweb
v.n. To move like a spider, &c. (i.q. makṛānā, q.v.).

def: (intens. n. fr. makr, q.v.), = P مکاره makkāra (for A. مکارة makkārat, fem. of makkār), adj. & s.m. & f. False, deceitful, insidious, artful, crafty; — a pretender, feigner; a cheat, knave, an impostor.

(for A. مکارة makkārat, fem. of makkār), = A مکار makkār (intens. n. fr. makr, q.v.), adj. & s.m. & f. False, deceitful, insidious, artful, crafty; — a pretender, feigner; a cheat, knave, an impostor.

ety: makkār, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Deceitfulness; artifice; artfulness, craftiness; imposture; cheating, knavery, roguery, fraud.

ety: makkār, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Deceitfulness; artfulness, craftiness, &c. ( = makkārī, q.v.).

ety: Prk. मक्वओ; S. मक्ष+कः; see makkhī
def: s.m. A large fly; a blow-fly; gad-fly; horse-fly.

ety: Prk. मकवणं; S. म्रक्षणं, rt. म्रक्ष्
def: s.m. Butter (see mākhan)
subs: s.f. A species of bean, Canavalia gladiata
s.m. A name of Krishṇ; — a proper name
(-se, or -kā), To make butter, to churn.

def: s.f. A fly; — the sight (of a gun)
subs: (or makkhiyāṅ) uṛānā or jhalnā (-par-se), To drive away flies (from off); — to perform servile offices (for); to flatter, to fawn (upon); — to have ulcers on the body (so as to be constantly employed in driving away the flies from them); — to fritter away the time, to trifle, to idle
(-par-se), To knock off a fly (from a thing or place, with a ball or an arrow), to be a good marksman
s.m. lit. 'One who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the food or drink into which it fell'; a miser, niggard, skinflint
s.m. One who kills a fly or flies; — a dirty, fllthy fellow; an idler, a lazy fellow; — one who makes an exact copy of a document, &c., mistakes, and blots, &c. included
To kill flies; to be unemployed, to idle, to trifle, to fool (syn. makkhī uṛānā)
(-par), Flies to buzz (about), or to sit (upon); — to be disgusting, or ugly, &c.
To swallow a living fly; — to bring evil upon oneself; — to tell great lies
Not to allow a fly to sit on the nose; — to shrink from incurring an obligation.

def: s.m. (dialec.) Honey.
s.f. (dialec.) Report, rumour, gossip.
s.f. (dialec.) = makkā, and makāʼī, qq.v.
adj. Of or from Mecca.
s.m. A measure containing a ṣāʻ and a half; or half a rat̤l, q.v.; or three kailajāt = 1⅞ manā.

ety: pass. part. of قلب 'to change, to turn,' &c.
def: part. adj. Changed, turned; turned upwards; turned upside-down, inverted; converted; transposed; — subverted; reversed
subs: s.m. A word, or a sentence, which may be read backwards or forwards (as درد; or the Eng. 'madam'); a palindrome
s.m. A sentence, or the like, having the order of its words changed by inversion, or by any transposition.

ety: pass. part. of کنّ 'to cover; to conceal,' &c.
def: part. adj. Concealed, hidden; secret
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَکْنُون (maknūn), from Arabic مَكْنُون (maknūn).

ety: prob. S. मार्कवः; Pers. also mako
def: s.m. The Cape gooseberry, or Brazil cherry, or 'winter cherry,' Physalis Peruviana, Nat. or. Solanaceae; (in Bengal and Eastern districts, it is called tipārī); — the medicinal plant Solanum rubrum; — Sarsaparilla.
s.m. (dialec.) = mako, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = mako, q.v.
s.f. Chalk.
s.m. (f. -ī), A large ant; an insect (cf. kīṛe-makoṛe).

ety: inf. n. of مکر 'to plot; to deceive,' &c.
def: s.m. Plotting; plot, machination; fraud, deceit; duplicity; wile, artifice, trick; cheating, imposture; evasion; pretext, pretence; colour; disguise
subs: s.m. Fraud and subterfuge; artifices, wiles, tricks, &c.
or kar-jānā, or makr gāṅṭhnā, v.n. To feign, pretend, sham.

ety: for makkaṛā = Prk. मक्कडओ; S. मर्कटकः
def: s.m. A large spider; a spider; — a tall, lean person; — a kind of grain (cf. makkā)
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Cobwebbed; formed like a cobweb
v.n. To move like a spider, &c. (i.q. makṛānā, q.v.).

ety: for A. مکرمة, prob. n. of place fr. کرم 'to excel in honour'
def: s.f. Honour, glory, nobleness, nobility, respectability; generosity, bounty, grace, beneficence.

ety: makaṛ or makkaṛ, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. अपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To move like a spider; to move in a winding path or course, to move crookedly or tortuously; to walk affectedly or with a swagger (syn. akṛānā); — to fidget; — to waver; — to be averse (to); to act contrary (to); to counteract; — to do (work, &c.) perfunctorily, or carelessly, or lazily (syn. jī ćurānā).
s.m. (dialec.) A dried stalk of Indian corn (cf. makkā).
s.f. A spider
subs: s.m. A spider's web; cobweb; — (met.) anything very fine and slender
To be touched by a spider and have eruptions on the skin.

def: adj. Deceitful, fraudulent, &c.
v.t. To wet, damp, moisten.

ety: pass. part. of کره 'to hate, detest, dislike,' &c.
def: part. adj. Hated, detested, disliked, disapproved; hateful, odious, abominable, detestable, execrable, loathsome, disgusting, foul, obscene; — distasteful, unpleasant; improper, unbecoming; — s.m. An act which the (Muhammadan) law disapproves of, but does not invalidate
subs: v.n. To be disliked, or disapproved, &c.

def: s.m. pl. (of makrūh), Odious or disgusting things, abominations, shameful acts; dirty or obscene things, &c.; acts disapproved by law; things to be avoided.
part. adj. Placed under embargo; seized, attached (in law), confiscated, sequestered.
part. adj. Placed under embargo; seized, attached (in law), confiscated, sequestered.

ety: pass. part. of قرن 'to join, unite,' &c.
def: part. adj. Joined, conjoined, fastened, bound together; adhering; connected; related; — near; contiguous; neighbouring.
part. adj. Cut; — lent; — burdened with debt (qarẓ); indebted; in debt.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْرُوض (maqrūz), from Arabic مَقْرُوض (maqrūḍ).

def: s.m. One who is in debt.

(inf. n. of مکس 'to offer a price; to bid (at a sale),' &c.), s.m.(?) Bidding (at a sale); beating down, lowering (a price), cheapening; — decrease; detriment; — oppressing; oppression; defrauding; iniquity; — accumulating money; collecting tribute, tolls, taxes, or tithes.
(inf. n. of مکث 'to delay'), s.m.(?) Delaying; waiting; — delay.

ety: inf. n. (used in the sense of maqṣūd
def: s.m. Intended sense (of), meaning, purport; — thing aimed at, or intended, or purposed; object of aim or pursuit; intention, design, purpose; desire, wish; object, aim, scope
subs: or maqs̤ad honā (-kā, or merā), (One's) object to be accomplished or attained; (one's) wish to be gained
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْصَد (maqsad), borrowed from Arabic مَقْصَد (maqṣad).

def: s.m. Part, portion.
s.m. Hypocrisy; — wrath; — multitude.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.) = makkhī, q.v.
s.f. A fly; a bee (i.q. makkhī, q.v.).
s.f. = مکشکا makshikā, q.v.
s.m. Honey; — a kind of honey-like mineral substance or pyrites (of which there are various kinds)
subs: vulg. mākshikaj, s.m. Bees'-wax (lit. 'produced from honey').

def: part. adj. Uncovered; discovered, detected; disclosed; displayed; revealed; — open.

ety: n. of place fr. قسم 'to divide,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of partition.

ety: for A. مکسوبة maksūbat, fem. of maksūb, pass. part. of کسب 'to acquire,' &c.
def: part. adj. Acquired, gained, gotten, earned.

ety: pass. part. of قصد 'to intend, desire,' &c.
def: part. adj. Intended, meant; purposed, designed; proposed; desired, wished, sought; — s.m. Intent, intention, design, purpose, drift, aim, view, desire, object, scope (syn. maqṣad).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْصُود (maqsūd), from Arabic مَقْصُود (maqṣūd).

def: part. adj. Divided, distributed, portioned out, apportioned, allotted; — s.m. Portion, share; allotment; lot, fate, destiny; (in Arith.) the dividend
subs: s.m. Createst common divisor or factor, the G.C.M.
It is a matter of fate or destiny.

def: part. adj. Broken; cut; — marked with the vowel kasra (a consonant); — s.m. (in Arith.) A fraction; — a consonant marked with the vowel kasra (ِ).
ex: first letter [is followed by a] kasra
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مکسور (maksūr), from Arabic مَكْسُور (maksūr).

ety: for A. مقصوره maqṣūrat, fem. of maqṣūr, pass. part. of قصر 'to confine, restrict,' &c.
def: part. adj. Confined, restricted; — abridged, abbreviated, curtailed, diminished; — s.m. (for alif-ě-maqṣūra), The abbreviated or short alif (as in دنييٰٰ or دنيا dunyā).

ety: for A. مکسورة maksūrat, fem. of maksūr
def: part. adj. = maksūr.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, of قطع 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. Cutting, severing, dividing; amputation; a cut; — interruption, an abrupt breaking off; a place of separation: a pause (in reading verse), a caesura; — the last verse of a poem (in which the writer introduces his nom de plume)
subs: (vulg. maqte-kā) band, Chorus of a song.
wety: Borrowed from Persian مَقْطَع (maqta').

r: maktab, madrasa, maktab baiṭhānā, baiṭhā-denā, meṅ, maktab-paṛhānā, maktab-ḵẖāna
ety: n. of place fr. کتب 'to write'
def: s.m. School-house, school; — a gambling-house
subs: s.m. pl. The boys of a school; school-boys.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَکْتَب (maktab), froml Arabic مَكْتَب (maktab).

ety: n. of place fr. قتل 'to kill'
def: s.m. Place of slaughter, or of execution; — place of death, any part of the body which, being wounded, causes death; mortal spot, vital part (syn. maram).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْتَل (maqtal), itself from Arabic مَقْتَل (maqtal). First attested as Middle Hindi مَقْتَل (maqtal) in 1503 A.D.

def: part. adj. Written; — sewn or stitched, or pasted, together; — s.m. What is written; a writing; a letter, an epistle; — a collection of letters (generally pasted together) forming a long roll
subs: s.m. Person written to; addressee; drawee.

def: s.m. pl. (of maktūb), Writings; letters (syn. makātīb).
adj. Epistolary; — delivered in writing.
part. Killed, slain, slaughtered; — s.m. One who is slain; — a lover
s.f. The being killed or slain.

ety: pass. part. of کتم 'to hide, conceal'
def: part. adj. Hidden, concealed.
adv. (dialec.) = baran, or varam, qq.v.

ety: pass. part. of عقل 'to be or become intelligent,' &c.
def: adj. Perceived by the intellect, intellectual; excogitated (opp. to manqūl, 'desumed,' or 'borrowed,' as is the science of the fundamentals of religion, and the like); — intelligible; perceptible; — understood; — approved by the intellect; approved; acceptable; reasonable; probable; just, right, proper; fair; decent; liberal; sufficient; satisfactory; — pertinent, apposite, appropriate, to the purpose; — formed in wisdom; good, nice, choice; — s.f. Any branch of knowledge that is excogitated (the opp. of manqūl, see above); philosophy, &c.; — intj. Right! just! all right. very good! very well! &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Seeing (or one who sees) what is acceptable, or reasonable, &c.; judicious; reasonable; — a judicious, or a reasonable, person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Speaking (or one who speaks) reasonably, or aptly, or properly, &c.; conversable
s.f. Pertinent, or proper, speaking or speech.

def: part. Eaten, devoured; — s.m. What is eaten, food, meat, victuals.

ety: for A. معقولة maʻqūlat, fem. of maʻqūl
def: part. adj. = maʻqūl, q.v.; — s.m. That which is understood, &c.; the understood, the intelligible.

ety: for A. مقولة maqūlat, fem. of maqūl, pass. part. of قال (for قول
def: part. adj. & s.m. Said; repeated over and over again; — what is said, a word; a saying; an adage, a proverb, a maxim, an aphorism; — a quotation
subs: (-kā), To mention or repeat a saying (of), to quote (from).

def: s.m. pl. (of mākūl), Eatables, victuals, viands.
s.m. pl. (of maʻqūl), Things perceived by the intellect, intellectual things; intelligible things; — things approved by the intellect, reasonable things, or words; probabilities, &c.
s.m. pl. of maqūla, q.v.

ety: maʻqūl, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Reasonableness; probability, &c. (see maʻqūl).

ety: for A. معقولية maʻqūlīyat, abst. s. fr. maʻqūl
def: s.f. Reasonableness; probability, &c. (see maʻqūl).

ety: pass. part. of عکس 'to invert, reverse; to transpose,' &c.
def: part. adj. Inverted; reversed; turned upside down; converted; transposed; — reflex; — perverted (in order, or in meaning)
subs: v.n. To be or become inverted, or reversed, or turned upside down.

def: s.m. Riches, money, property, wealth, possessions, merchandise, stock, goods, effects, estate; rent or revenue (from land); — finance; — a prize (in a lottery, &c.); any great thing, a something, a thing of importance or consequence (e.g. wǒh kyā māl hai ki maiṅ usko girā na sakūṅgā); a dainty, tit-bit; — square (of a number, e.g. 9 is the māl of 3); — indigo dye, manufactured indigo, or the plant in the leaf and bundle (opp. to pāb), the granulated sediment in an indigo vat after heating and drawing off the water
subs: To squander wealth, &c.; — to feed on dainties
s.m. The square of a square, the fourth power
adj. Heaped, brimful; full, replete, abundant; — māl-ā-māl karnā, v.t. To heap up; to fill to the brim; — to enrich
s.m. Property left by a deceased person; unappropriated or unclaimed property
s.m. Export; — recovered property ( = māl bar-āmada); — property left by a criminal ( = māl guẕāshtaʼě mujrim)
Balance of exports and imports, balance of trade
vulg. māl-ḵẖāwind, s.m. A man of property; a proprietor
adj. & s.m. Wealthy, rich, moneyed; — a wealthy person; a proprietor
s.m. A wealthy man; a proprietor
s.m. A son of a whore, a pimp or pander
s.m. Misoellaneous revenues (from customs and other sources, exclusive of land); duties
s.m. Joint or undivided property; joint stock
s.m. A person who stands security (for property, or money, in opp. to ḥāẓir-ẓāmin), a surety, bondsman (for the payment of rent or a debt)
s.f. Security (for rent or debt); pecuniary bail; security in property
Property to be (cut off, or) stolen; — goods to be sold in large quantities (baṛā being generally expressed, e.g. wahāṅ baṛā māl kaṭtā hai)
s.f. Goods'-van, luggage-van; — luggage (or goods') train
adj. & s.m. Paying rent or taxes; — one who pays revenue to (or holds land under) government, a landholder, landlord, a manager of cultivation and village matters, a tenant, a subject
s.f. Land-revenue; rent paid in money; — the paying of rent and taxes; — the state of a renter; — assessed land
or māl-ě-maurūs̤a, s.m. A bequest, legacy
s.m. 'Criminal and property'; a criminal with property
s.m. A consumer of the property of others; a swindler; one who embezzles; one who borrows and never pays
adj. Intoxicated with wealth, purse-proud, boastful
(or muft-kā māl), s.m. Property acquired without cost or labour
s.m. Property devoted to religious purposes
Unclaimed property; — anything bought cheap or got for nothing
s.m. A valuable prize; a dainty, tit-bit, delicacy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

def: s.m. A wrestler, professional boxer, prize-fighter; an athlete, a champion, a hero (syn. pahlawān).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: Prk. मल्लं; S. माल्यं; — or S. माला
def: s.f. The staff-tree, Celastrus paniculatus, or C. nutans (the aromatic bitter seeds yield an empyreumatic oil (Oleum nigrum) which is used in lamps; and in medicine, having been found a successful remedy in beriberi, rheumatism, and paralysis, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: rt. of mālīdan; — rt. Zend mared = S. मर्द् (मृद्)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Wiping, rubbing; twisting; crushing; — rubber, wiper, napkin, towel (used as last member of compounds; e.g. gosh-māl, rū-māl, &c., qq.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

def: s.m. Dirt, filth; dust; impurity; impure matter; excrement; refuse; sediment; dregs; scum; alloy or dross (of metal); rust; — defilement, moral impurity, sin; — any excretion of the body, e.g. adeps, marrow, serum, semen, blood, fæces, mucus (of the nose), wax (of the ear), sweat, rheum, phlegm, tears, dandruff; — camphor; cuttle-fish bone
subs: s.m. A piece of cloth worn over the privities
or mal-hārī, adj. Filth-removing; taking away impurity, &c.
s.f. The removal or destruction of filth, or impurity, &c.

def: s.f. A wreath, garland, chaplet (of flowers, &c.); — a row, line; a chain, string (of beads, &c.), a necklace, a Hindū rosary; a collection, group, a collection of words arranged in order, a vocabulary, a dictionary; a book; — a field; — a cocoa-nut shell
subs: s.m. The seed of the Elœocarpus ganitrus (of which rosaries are made)
or mālā japnā, To tell (one's) beads
s.m. (f. -ī), A wreath-maker, garland-weaver; a florist, gardener, flower-seller
adj. (f. -vatī), Having a wreath or garland, garlanded, wreathed, crowned.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit माला (mālā).

ety: perf. part. of malnā; — and = Prk. मलिअओ; S. मृदित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Rubbed, scrubbed, &c.; worn; laid bare; effaced
subs: adj. & s.m. & f. 'With the five marks effaced,' turned five years (a horse); over eight, or ten, years; aged; — a horse over five years, &c. (cf. dale-panj).

def: s.m. Name of a district lying west and south-west of Bengal; — name of a barbarous tribe of people; a man of that tribe; — an epithet of Vishṇu.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: for A. malʼā, fem. of malʼānu, v.n. fr. مَلِئَي 'to be full'
def: adj. Full.
s.m. The country Malabar
s.m. The Malaya mountains (or a mountain in that range) in Malabar (answering to the western Ghāṭs; it is from this range that the best sandal-wood is brought).
s.m. = malāgir, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. & s.m. = malagīrī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Of the colour of sandal-wood; — the colour of sandal-wood; — the best sandal-wood; — name of a hill-bird.
s.m. A bulrush.

ety: for A. ملاحة, inf. n. of ملح 'to be salt'
def: s.f. The being salt, or savoury; the being good, agreeable, or elegant; goodness; elegance; beauty; excellence; fineness; delicacy; — beauty of the mouth; — piquant look or appearance; a rich brown (or dark) complexion.
s.m. pl. (of mulḥid), Heretics; infidels, atheists.
s.f. Scum (of soup, or boiled rice, or of any liquid); cream; clotted cream; — a kind of dish made of milk
s.f. Rubbing; scouring; rubbing down (a horse, &c.); — price paid for rubbing, or scrubbing, &c.

ety: prob. mal(nā)+Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य + ता + इका
def: s.f. Rich alluvial soil on the bank of a river; meadow-land.
(pl. of ملك malak), = P ملائکه malāʼika (for A. ملائکة malāʼikat), s.m. pl. Angels.
(for A. ملائکة malāʼikat), = A ملائِك malāʼik (pl. of ملك malak), s.m. pl. Angels.
s.m. pl. (of malʻūn), The cursed, or accursed.

ety: for ملأك malʼak, fr. لأك, whence الأك 'to send'; — Heb. מלאך
def: s.m. An angel
subs: s.m. The angel of death (called ʻIzrāʼil, or ʻIzrāʼīl).

def: s.f. (dialec.) Doubt, suspicion, apprehension.
s.f. A garland.
s.f. A garland; a necklace, &c. ( = mālā, q.v.); a row, series; a multitude; — the double jasmine; — an intoxicating drink; — a daughter; — a palace.
s.f. A female messenger, a confidante; — an amorous woman, a lewd woman; — a female elephant.
s.m. = malāḵẖā, q.v.

ety: for A. ملکة malakat, inf. n. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.
def: s.m. Possession; — an acquired habit or principle; — quickness of apprehension or perception; strength of intellect; intellect, genius; — proficiency; mastery.
s.f. pl. (of ملکة malakat, inf. n. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.), Possessions; qualities, endowments; habits, principles.
s.f. contrac. of malkāhaṭ, q.v.
s.m. A locust (syn. ṭiḍḍī).
s.m. Malacca; — adj. Malay, Malayan.
adj. Angelic

ety: for A. ملکية malakīyat, fem. of malakī
def: adj. = malakī, q.v.

ety: malak˚, prob. = Prk. मलक्क(इ), or मलक्के(इ), fr. S. मल+कृ
def: v.n. (dialec.) To feel nausea, to be sick at stomach; to be bilious; — to be troubled with doubt, or suspicion; to be apprehensive (of).
v.n. To walk like a kahār or chairman, &c. (trotting and raising the shoulders); — to walk affectedly, to perk oneself, to strut.

ety: malak, q.v.+ūt, a termn. borrowed fr. the Aramaic
def: s.f. Kingdom, empire, dominion; — (for ʻālam-ě-malakūt), the world of angels or spirits; the heavenly court; — angels; spirits.

ety: inf. n. of ملّ 'to be tired,' &c.
def: s.m. Weariness, fatigue, languor, ennui; dejection, sadness, melancholy; affliction, grief, anguish; vexation, displeasure
subs: (-ko), Languor, &c. to come (upon); to be seized with sadness, &c.

ety: for A. ملالة, inf. n. of ملّ 'to be weary,' &c.
def: s.f. = malāl, q.v.

r: malām, malāmat, malāmat karnā, par, ko
def: (inf. n. of لام (for لوم) 'to reprove,' &c.), s.m. = P ملامت malāmat (for A. ملامة, inf. n. of لام 'to reprove'), s.f. Reproof, rebuke, censure, reprehension, reproach, accusation, blame; reviling; disgrace; opprobrium; contumely
adj. See s.v. māl, 'wealth.'

r: malāmat, malām, malāmat karnā, par, ko
def: (for A. ملامة, inf. n. of لام 'to reprove'), s.f. = A ملام malām (inf. n. of لام (for لوم) 'to reprove,' &c.), s.m. Reproof, rebuke, censure, reprehension, reproach, accusation, blame; reviling; disgrace; opprobrium; contumely
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَلَامَت (malāmat), from Arabic مَلَامَة (malāma).

def: adj. Reproached; reprehensible, blamable.

r: malāmatī, malāmat, malāmatī denā, ko, malāmat karnā
def: s.f. corr. of malāmat, q.v.
s.m. A rubbing; grinding; threshing, &c.; — a path, track (syn. līk).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मिलन (milana).

def: v.t. To cause to rub, or to scour, or to grind; — (for malwānā) to cause to be rubbed, &c.
wety: Causative of ملنا (milnā).

def: s.m. A kind of Moḥammadan derwish, who lets the hair of his head grow to its full length, and leaves it uncombed and dishevelled; — a derwish in a state of ecstasy; a religious enthusiast; — a careless or inconsiderate person; — a tall, robust fellow; — a kind of bird.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ملنگ (malang, “jovial, lively, ecstasied, tipsy”).

def: s.m. A kind of Moḥammadan derwish, who lets the hair of his head grow to its full length, and leaves it uncombed and dishevelled; — a derwish in a state of ecstasy; a religious enthusiast; — a careless or inconsiderate person; — a tall, robust fellow; — a kind of bird.
s.m. A labourer employed in manufacturing salt, a salt-maker.
s.f. A wanton woman.
v.n. = malār gānā, q.v.
s.f. Name of a rāginī or particular musical mode or scale.
s.f. = mālsrī, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) Pasteboard.

ety: imperf. part. of malnā, q.v.; — ˚at = Prk. अंतं = S. अत्
def: s.m. A worn rupee, or other coin; — the bare or smooth part (of a coin, where the impression has worn off, or been rubbed off)
subs: v.t. To debase (coin) by rubbing smooth, &c.

def: s.f. A bud, blossom; — a maid, virgin, young woman; — moonshine, moonlight; night; — the name of several plants and flowers, e.g. Jasminum grandiflorum; Bignonia suaveolens; Echites caryophyllata; Gaertnera racemosa, &c.
subs: s.f. The creeping plant, Gaertnera racemosa
(or basant-māltī), s.f. A kind of powder given in consumption (of which the principal ingredients are gold leaves and pearls).

def: s.m. The country of Malwa (in Central India); — the inhabitants of Malwa
subs: or mālav-śrī, s.f. A particular rāginī or musical mode or scale.

def: s.f. The plant Ipomæa (or Convolvulus) turpethum (com. called teʼorī).

s.m. Name of a mountain, or of a mountainous range, in Malabar; the western Ghāṭs in the Dekkan, or peninsula of India (from these mountains the finest sandal-wood is brought); — name of the country (Malayālam or Malabār) which lies to the east of the Malaya range; — the garden of Indra; a celestial grove; — (S. malayās), name of one of the minor Dvīpas or divisions of the world
subs: vulg. malayaj, s.m. lit. 'Growing on the Malaya mountains'; a sandal-tree; sandal-wood
(˚ya+ać), s.m. The Malaya mountains, answering to the western Ghāṭs
or malayā-giri, or malayā-gīr, s.m. = malayāćal, q.v.

def: s.m. Name of a mountain, or of a mountainous range, in Malabar; the western Ghāṭs in the Dekkan, or peninsula of India (from these mountains the finest sandal-wood is brought); — name of the country (Malayālam or Malabār) which lies to the east of the Malaya range; — the garden of Indra; a celestial grove; — (S. malayās), name of one of the minor Dvīpas or divisions of the world
subs: vulg. malayaj, s.m. lit. 'Growing on the Malaya mountains'; a sandal-tree; sandal-wood
(˚ya+ać), s.m. The Malaya mountains, answering to the western Ghāṭs
or malayā-giri, or malayā-gīr, s.m. = malayāćal, q.v.

def: s.m. See s.v. malaya.
s.m. See s.v. malaya.

ety: n. of place fr. لاذ (for لوذ
def: s.m. An asylum, a refuge, protection, defence, stronghold; — a protector.

ety: Prk. मलेअव्वअं; S. मर्दितव्य+कं, rt. मृद्; or fr. S. मल
def: s.m. The rubbish of a fallen building, or wall, &c.; the stones, brick, mortar, &c. lying jumbled together; — rubbish, litter, refuse, sweepings, dirt
part. adj. Clothed, clad, dressed; — s.m. Clothes (which have been worn), garment, wearing apparel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَلْبُوس (malbūs), from Arabic مَلْبُوس (malbūs).

def: s.m. pl. (of malbūs), Clothes, garments.
adj. (dialec.) Worn before; usually put on, in ordinary wear.
s.m. Rubbing; grinding; pressing, squeezing
subs: v.t. To rub; grind; press, squeeze; — to rub down (a horse, &c.); to thresh (corn, &c.); to beat.

def: s.m. The country of Malabār or Malayālam.
s.m. The country of Malabār or Malayālam.
adj. See s.v. malā (perf. part. of malnā).
s.m. The country of Malabār (in the Dekkan).
s.m. The country of Malabār (in the Dekkan).

ety: pass. part. of لفّ 'to involve, to wrap,' &c.
def: part. adj. Involved, wrapt up, enveloped, enclosed; folded (as a letter); — collected, gathered; — an enclosure (in a letter, &c.); an envelope; — anything wrapped up or folded (as a cabbage, &c.).

ety: pass. part. of لفظ 'to throw out; to utter,' &c.
def: s.m. That which is spoken or uttered (by); saying; saying of a saint or a spiritual guide; — annals.
s.m. pl. (of malfūz̤), Utterances sayings; — annals.

ety: S. मल+गञ्ज+कं; cf. argajā = S. अष्ट+गंज+कं
def: adj. Dirty, filthy, foul; soiled; — rumpled, ruffled; — black and blue; — (dialec.) brackish.

ety: for malgobā = mal, 'dirt, &c.'+gobā = gowā, fr. Prk. गोवे(इ) = S. गोपय(ति), 'to secrete,' caus. of rt. गुप्
def: s.m. Entrails; garbage; — matter (from a sore); — anything that emits saliva or viscous matter; — a thick infusion; — a kind of dish, māsh or pulse mixed with dahī, q.v.; — dirt, sweepings, rubbish.
s.m. See ملغوبا malg̠obā.
s.m. = P مالگذاري māl-guẕārī, s.f. See s.v. māl, 'wealth.'
s.f. = P مالگذار māl-guẕār, s.m. See s.v. māl, 'wealth.'
s.m. corr. of marham, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = mallār or mallārī, q.v.
subs: s.m. The sound of the mallār.

ety: pass. part. of لحظ 'to look through half-shut eyes,' &c.
def: part. adj. Regarded, considered, contemplated; looked at kindly, viewed with affection, regard, or respect
subs: v.t. To keep in regard; to bear in mind; to consider, &c.

def: s.m. The caste of gardeners; a man of that caste, a gardener, a florist.
adj. (f. -inī) = malin, q.v.
adj. Of or belonging to property, or wealth, or merchandise, &c.; wealthy; — of or belonging to the revenue; financial; civil (as opp. to military)
adj. & s.m. = mleććh, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of mālīdan, and = Zend marsta, rt. mared; S. मर्दित or मृदित, rt. मृद्; cf. māl
def: part. Rubbed; crushed, &c.; — s.m. Bread rubbed into crumbs, and then mixed with sugar and butter, and again rubbed well together with the hands; a cake made of bread so rubbed, or of pounded meal mixed with butter and sugar; — a fine woollen cloth (made of Kashmīr lamb's wool).
s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

ety: contrac. of mālīda, perf. part. of māl-īdan, 'to rub,' &c.
def: s.m. A cake made of pounded meal (or of flour), milk, butter, and sugar; — a kind of fine woollen cloth (made of Kashmīr lamb's-wool).

ety: v.n. fr. ملح 'to salt,' &c.; see milḥ
def: adj. Beautiful; agreeable, sweet, charming, piquant; of dark-brown colour, nut-brown (syn. sāṅʼolā).
s.m. Possessor, proprietor, occupier, owner, master, employer, lord, ruler; the Supreme Being; — a husband
subs: s.m. Holder of a share or shares, shareholder
s.m. The real owner; the proprietor de facto
v.t. To make (one) the proprietor or owner (of), &c.; to empower, entitle, give a claim (to)
s.m. A kind of water-fowl, a bittern (?)
s m. 'Lord of the territory or kingdom,' a title addressed to sovereigns, princes, prime-ministers, &c.
s.m. Holder of a bill or draft
(-kā), To be or become the owner or proprietor (of), to own, to have a title (to).

def: s.m. A garland-maker, a gardener, florist, flower-gatherer
subs: colourist, dyer; — a sort of bird.

def: s.m. A king (i.q. malik, q.v.).

ety: said to be contrac. fr. mālik, act. part. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.
def: s.m. A king; a title of honour or respect (in India it is com. given to camel-men, prob. as signifying 'possessor, owner')
subs: s.m. Prince of merchants, a title given to the chief merchant in a city
s.m. Prince of poets; poet laureate (syn. rāj-kavi).

ety: for A. ملکة malikat, fem. of malik, q.v.
def: s.f. A queen
s.m. pl. (of mālik), Proprietors, owners, masters, &c.

ety: mālik, q.v.+āna = Zend ana, or āna = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Proprietary; like an owner; — in the manner of an owner; — s.m. An annual or monthly allowance paid to a zamīndār by the person who occupies his lands, whether the state or a jagīr-dār; an allowance to a zamīndār ousted from his estate
subs: s.m. Fees levied on cultivators by a landholder for his household expenses

def: s.m. Melancholy; madness, insanity.
adj. Royal, princely, lordly.

ety: for A. مالکية, abst. s. fr. mālikī, fr. mālik
def: s.f. State of master or owner, mastership, ownership, lordship, proprietorship, proprietary right; possession.
adj. Apparent; public.
adj. = ملن malin, q.v.
s.f. The wife of a mālī or gardener; — a female gardener or flower-seller.
s.f. (of malina), A dirty or filthy woman; — a woman during menstruation.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀺𑀮𑀤𑀺 (miladi), from Sanskrit मिलति (milati).

def: adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), Dirty, filthy, foul, impure, soiled, unclean; tarnished, stained, sullied; tainted; rusty; polluted, depraved, sinful, bad, vile, vicious; — black, dark, obscure; obscured, beclouded; dull, dim; — troubled, disturbed (in mind), indisposed, vexed, concerned; sad, heavy, downcast; melancholy
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a depraved or polluted mind, troubled or disturbed, &c. in mind; — one who has a polluted mind, &c.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Dirty-faced; black-faced, dark-faced; — low, vile, wicked; cruel, fierce, savage; — a black-faced person; a cruel person; a savage; — a ghost, an apparition; an evil spiri t, a goblin; — a black-faced monkey, a baboon; — fire; an epithet of Agni.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मिलन (milana).

def: s.f. Dirtiness, filthiness, foulness; filth; sediment; impurity; vileness, wickedness; blackness.
s.f. The wife of a mālī or gardener; — a female gardener or flower-seller.
s.f. The plant Hedysarum alhagi; — the shrub Echitis caryophyllata; — name of a rāginī or musical mode; — an epithet of Durgā or Pārvatī; and of the celestial Ganges; — a species of Sanskrit metre.
s.f. = malinā, q.v.
s.m. Foulness, uncleanness, dirtiness, pollution, defilement, impurity; sinfulness.
s.f. Rubbing; kneading; polishing, furbishing; friction; polish; shampooing, tripsis
subs: v.t. To rub, &c.; to polish; — v.n. To work or heave (as the stomach under a feeling of nausea); to feel nausea (e.g. jī mālish kar-rahā hai).

def: s.f. (m. malishṭha), An exceedingly dirty or filthy woman; — a woman during menstruation; — a very vile woman.
adj. (superlat. or intens. of mala), Excessively dirty, very filthy, very foul, very polluted; very wicked; — s.m. An excessively dirty or filthy person; an exceedingly vile person.
s.m. = māliyat, q.v.

ety: Prk. मल्लिओ; S. मल्लिकः; — or fr. malnā, q.v.
def: s.m. A small vessel, made of wood or cocoanut-shell, for holding oil.

ety: for an unused A. مالية mālīyat, abst. s. fr. mālī
def: s.f. Wealth, opulence; matter of money; — value, worth; — revenue, tribute (e.g. adāʼě māliya bādshāhī-se munḥarif ho-gayā); finance, exchequer
subs: (-kī), To estimate the value (of), to value, estimate.

ety: n. of place fr. لجأ 'to flee (to
def: s.m. A place of refuge, an asylum; a retreat; — security; support.
s.f. (dialec.) Nausea at stomach; excess of bile; bile; — doubt, suspicion, apprehension (i.q. malak).

ety: prob. malik, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. (dialec.) To talk, or to act, grandly, or affectedly (the word is com. to the dialect of women).
s.m. A caste of Hindūs forcibly converted to Muhammadanism.
s.f. See s.v. māl, 'wreath.'
s.m. The upright post in a sugar-mill, &c.

ety: mal, fr. malnā, q.v.+kham or khambhā, q.v.
def: s.m. The upright post in a sugar-mill.
s.m. (dialec.) A hero.
s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode.
s.f. An athletic woman; a woman of the Mall caste; — Arabian jasmine.

s.m. A professional wrestler or boxer (the offspring of an outcast Kshatriya by a Kshatriya woman), a pancratiast; prize-fighter; an athlete; a gymnast; a tumbler; an acrobat; an athletic person; — a vessel for drinking, a cup; — the cheek and temple, the hemicranium; — the residue of an oblation; — adj. Strong, stout, robust, athletic; — good, excellent, best, chief
subs: s.f. Wrestling-ground, place for athletic contests, a palaestra, an arena; a gym. nasium; — the site of any conflict, battle-field
or mal-jī, s.m. A respectful form of address to a Hindū gentleman; — Sir Athlete, &c.
s.f. A train or procession of wrestlers; — a wrestling-match; boxing-match
or mal-yudh, s.m. Pugilistic encounter, wrestling-match; prize-fight; athletic contest; rough fight; wrestling, boxing, fighting

def: s.m. A sea-man, sailor, mariner; a boatman, waterman; — a manufacturer, or a vendor, of salt.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَلَّاح (mallāḥ).

def: s.f. The profession of a sailor; the business of a boatman; — seamanship, navigation; — fare paid for crossing a river, &c., boat-fare, passage-money
subs: or mallāḥī gālī, s.f. Boatmen's abuse; abusing (a person) without naming (him)
s.m. (Swimming) hand over hand, boatman-fashion (or as a dog swims).

def: s.f. Sailor's wife; boatman's wife; boat-woman.
s.f. Sailor's wife; boatman's wife; boat-woman.
s.m. corr. of marham, q.v.
s.f. Name of a rāginī, or musical mode, sung during the rains
subs: To sing a malār; — to be merry; — to dwell or expatiate (on).

def: s.f. See mallār.
s.f. Arabian jasmine, Jasminum zambac (a beautiful flowering shrub).
s.f. Arabian jasmine, Jasminum zambac; — name of a species of metre in Sanskrit prosody.
s.m. A monkey.
s.m. A bear.

ety: prob. fr. S. मृदु by redupl.; lit. 'very soft'
def: s.f. Muslin.
adj. (f. -ī), Salt, brackish; sad, downcast, depressed.

ety: malmalā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. वट्टी = S. वृत्तिः
def: s.f. Brackishness; — repining; sadness; depression
subs: (-ko), To suffer from sadness, or depression, or dejection; to repine.

ety: malmalā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. वट्टी = S. वृत्तिः
def: s.f. Brackishness; — repining; sadness; depression
subs: (-ko), To suffer from sadness, or depression, or dejection; to repine.

def: s.m. An intercalary (thirteenth) month (in which no religious ceremonies should be performed).
part. adj. Rubbed out, effaced; wiped out, wiped off; ruined, destroyed; — decided, disposed of (a dispute, &c.); hushed, quashed (a quarrel, &c.)
subs: v.t. To efface; to wipe out or off; to pull down, to raze, to destroy; — to quash, to hush up (a dispute, &c.)
v.n. To be rubbed out, to be effaced, &c.

ety: mal˚ = Prk. मल(इ) = S. मृद्ना(ति), rt. मृद्
def: v.t. To rub; to rub down (a horse); to scrub, scour; to furbish; to anoint; — to grind; to tread on, trample on; to tread out (corn); — to wring (the hands)
subs: v.t. To rub; to squeeze; to grind, to crush, &c. ( = malnā).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀺𑀮𑀤𑀺 (miladi), from Sanskrit मिलति (milati).

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. ملولة malūlat; see malūl
def: s.m. Vexation; grief, regret; affliction; sadness, dejection, melancholy
subs: (-ko), or malole uṭhnā (-meṅ), or malole khānā, To feel or suffer vexation, or grief, &c.; to be vexed; to be grieved, to grieve; to be sad, &c.
(-ke), Griefs, or sadness, &c. to be dissipated; to be consoled.

def: s.m. A kind of rich cake; a pancake.

ety: imperf. part. of malnā, q.v.; — ˚at = Prk. अंतं = S. अत्
def: s.m. A worn rupee, or other coin; — the bare or smooth part (of a coin, where the impression has worn off, or been rubbed off)
subs: v.t. To debase (coin) by rubbing smooth, &c.

ety: pass. part. of ألف 'to keep or cleave' (to
def: part. adj. Familiar, accustomed to live in friendship (with); habituated; clung (to); beloved (country, &c.), native (land); — ordinary, customary.

ety: pass. part. of علف 'to feed (a beast
def: part. adj. Fed with fodder or provender, foddered; — stall-fed; fatted.

ety: for A. مالوفة maʼlūfat, fem. of maʼlūf
def: adj. = mālūf, q.v.

ety: for A. معلوفة maʻlūfat, fem. of maʻlūf
def: part. adj. Fed with fodder or provender, foddered; — stall-fed; fatted.
s.m. = malūk, q.v.
adj. (rustic or dialec.) Beautiful, handsome.
s.m. A kind of worm.

ety: pass. part. of علّ 'to be or become diseased, or sick,' &c.
def: part. adj. Diseased, distempered, indisposed, sick, ill; weakened; caused, produced, effected; — s.m.(?) An effect (the opp. of ʻillat, q.v.); an inference.

ety: pass. part. of ملّ 'to be wearied,' &c.; see malāl
def: part. adj. Fatigued, tired, weary; languid; sick, indisposed; vexed; affected; grieved; sad, dejected, melancholy, out of spirits.
adj. corr. of معلول maʼlūl, q.v.
part. adj. Reprehended, reproached, blamed, accused.
part. adj. corr. of معلوم maʼlūm, q.v.
part. adj. Known; distinguished; celebrated, famous, notorious; clear, evident, certain, obvious, apparent; (in Geom.) given (as a line, &c.); — s.m. The active voice (of a verb)
subs: (-ko), To know, understand; to discover, find out; to perceive, to behold; to recognize; to feel; to deem, suppose
It is not known to me; I do not know
It appears, it seems; it is found (to be)
(-ko, or -par), To be or become known (to), to be discovered; to be deemed, or supposed; to appear, seem.
wety: From Arabic مَعْلُوم (maʕlūm) (through Classical Persian معلوم (ma'lūm)), from عَلِمَ (ʕalima).

ety: for A. معلومة maʻlūmat, fem. of maʻlūm
def: part. adj. = maʻlūm.
(pl. of maʻlūmat, fem. of maʻlūm), s.f. pl. Things known; sciences.
part. adj. Cursed, damned, accursed, execrated, detestable, excommunicated, driven out of society; shut out from mercy; — s.m. One who is cursed, &c.; the devil; an imp, a devil.

ety: for A. ملعونة malʻūnat, fem. of malʻūn
def: part. adj. Cursed, damned, accursed, execrated, detestable, excommunicated, driven out of society; shut out from mercy; — s.m. One who is cursed, &c.; the devil; an imp, a devil.
s.m. The country of Malwa ( = mālav).
s.f. Price paid for rubbing, or scrubbing, or scouring, &c.
s.m. One who rubs, or scrubs, or scours; a rubber, scrubber, &c.
v.t. To cause to be rubbed, or scoured, &c.; — to have, or to get (a thing) rubbed, &c.

ety: A. māl + P. aff. war, qq.v.
def: adj. Rich, wealthy, opulent.
s.m. A wreath, garland, chaplet, crown; a flower
subs: or mālya-vant, or mālya-vān, s.m. (lit. 'garlanded; crowned'), Name of a mountainous range (eastward of mount Meru) described as one of the smaller ranges of India proper.

ety: pass. part. of لزِم; see lāzim
def: part. adj. Connected; affixed; inseparable; — belonging (to).
s.f. Mother; — a respectful compellation for an old woman; — a woman who acts as a kind of general servant; a maid-servant
subs: s.f. The work of a maid-servant.

def: s.m. A maternal uncle; — (dialec.) a respectful compellation for a father-in-law, and also for a male person in general; — a snake; a rat (terms used euphemistically at night, when the use of the plain name is held to be unlucky).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit माम (māma), from Proto-Dravidian *māma.

def: s.f. Mother; — a respectful compellation for an old woman; — a woman who acts as a kind of general servant; a maid-servant
subs: s.f. The work of a maid-servant.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit माम (māma), from Proto-Dravidian *māma.

def: adj. Of me; my, mine (i.q. merā)
s.f. Midwifery.

ety: fr. mama, gen. sing. of ma
def: adj. & s.m. Mine, my; — selfish, greedy, covetous; — a miser, a niggard; — a maternal uncle.
s.f. A selfish or niggardly woman; — the wife of a maternal uncle, an aunt.

r: mamālik, mumālik, mamlakat, mamālik-ě-g̠air-āʼīn, mamālik-ě-maḥrūsa, mamālik-ě-mag̠ribī-o-shimālī, mamālik-ě-mufawwaẓa
def: corr. mumālik, s.m. pl. (of mamlakat, q.v.), Kingdoms, states, dominions, provinces, countries, regions
subs: Protected states, or provinces, or dominion

ety: n. of place fr. A. أمن 'to be or become secure or safe'
def: s.m. A place of security or safety; a place of freedom from fear.

ety: n. of place fr. مرّ 'to pass through or by'
def: s.m. Place of passage or transit; pass; passage; transit; ford; a canal; — (fig.) account, cause.
s.f. Death (i.q. maut); — place or time of death.
s.f. Affection; maternal affection, &c. (see mamatā).

s.f. Egoism; the sense of 'meum'; the interest, or affection, entertained for objects from the considering them as belonging to or connected with one's self; sense of ownership, or of self-interest; appropriation; selfishness; affection, attachment (to); — individuality; — self-sufficiency; pride, arrogance; covetousness, avarice
subs: vulg. mamtā-jukt, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Filled with selfishness, self-interested, selfish; miserly; — an egotist, a selfish man, a miser.

def: s.m. = mamatā, q.v.

ety: n. of place fr. نبع 'to spring or burst from' (as water from a fountain
def: s.m. Fountain, jet dʼeau; — fount, spring, source, origin; — a water-channel cut from a canal to a field.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian منبع (“spring”).

ety: pass. part. of مدّ 'to extend, to prolong'
def: part. adj. Extended, prolonged, protracted, lengthened out; long; — marked with the sign madda (ٓ)
subs: The alif marked with the sign madda; the lengthened alif, the long a (آ ā).

ety: for A. ممدودة mamdūdat, fem. of mamdūd
def: part. adj. Extended, prolonged, protracted, lengthened out; long; — marked with the sign madda (ٓ)
subs: The alif marked with the sign madda; the lengthened alif, the long a (آ ā).

def: part. adj. Praised, celebrated; famous; laudable, commendable; — above-mentioned, aforesaid; — s.m. One who is praised; the hero (of a poem, &c.)
(obl. pl. of mamdūḥ), s.m. pl. The praised; the famous.
adj. (f. -ī), Of or relating to a maternal uncle; maternal uncle's
subs: s.m. Maternal uncle's son, first cousin by the mother's side
s.f. Maternal uncle's daughter, first cousin, &c.

def: s.f. Selfishness; partiality; — wife of a maternal uncle, aunt
s.f. A selfish or niggardly woman; — the wife of a maternal uncle, an aunt.

ety: mamiyā = S माम+इकः+sās = Prk. सासू = S. श्वश्रूः
def: s.f. Maternal aunt of a husband or wife.
s.m. Maternal uncle of a husband or wife.
s.m. corr. of معامله muʻāmala, q.v.
part. adj. Filled; full.
part. adj. & s.m. Possessed; in one's power; — A purchased slave or captive; a Mamaluke

ety: for A. مملوکة mamlūkat, fem. of mamlūk
def: part. adj. & s.m. Possessed; in one's power; — A purchased slave or captive; a Mamaluke

ety: for A. مملکة, inf. n. of ملك 'to possess'
def: s.f. Empire, kingdom, realm, sovereignty, dominion, country, province, district, possession; — regal power, grandeur, magnificence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَمْلَکَت (mamlakat), from Arabic مَمْلَكَة (mamlaka). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi مملکت (mmlkt /⁠mamlakat⁠/).

def: part. adj. Prohibited; forbidden; restrained; — illicit; contraband.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَمْنُوع (mamnū'), borrowed from Arabic مَمْنُوع (mamnūʕ), from مَنَعَ (manaʕa).

ety: for A. ممنوعة mamnūʻat, fem. of mamnūʻ
def: part. adj. Prohibited; forbidden; restrained; — illicit; contraband.

ety: pass. part. of منّ 'to confer a benefit (on
def: part. adj. Who has received a favour, favoured, obliged; grateful, thankful
subs: v.t. To confer a favour on, to favour, to oblige
v.n. To be under an obligation (to, -kā), to feel obliged, to be grateful or thankful (for).

def: s.f. Obligation.
s.m. A wagtail, Motacilla, (syn. ṣaʻwa).
s.m. A twist; wreath; — ache, pain (particularly in the bowels).
s.f. (Dakh.), corr. of mahmez, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of مسخ 'to change the form of,' &c.
def: part. Changed, metamorphosed, deformed.
part. adj. Touched; tangible; — furious.
s.m. A maternal uncle, &c. ( = māmā, q.v.).
part. Hoped for, expected; — s.m. Hope.

ety: pass. part. of عمل 'to 'make; to do, act,' &c.
def: part. adj. Made, prepared; acted upon; (in Gram.) governed; — established, customary, wonted, usual; — s.m. Action, operation; — usage, established custom, practice; habit; — fixed allowance or gratuity
subs: A recurring period (for a thing or act); the usual period (of menstruation).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْمُول (ma'mūl), borrowed from Arabic مَعْمُول (maʕmūl), from عَمِلَ (ʕamila).

def: adj. Customary, practised, usual, ordinary; current (as expenditure); due (time).
s.m. = māmū = māmā, qq.v.

ety: perf. part. of أمن 'to be or become secure or safe'
def: part. Rendered secure or safe, preserved; placed in security, secured, safely deposited (money, &c.); secure, safe; freed (from), exempted; — firm, constant.

ety: for A. مامونة maʼmūnat, fem. of maʼmūn
def: adj. = māmūn, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of عمر 'to become inhabited, or peopled,' &c.
def: part. adj. Inhabited; peopled; well stocked with people, populous; colonized, cultivated, well-cultivated; in a flourishing state, flourishing; affluent, prosperous, happy; delightful; — in sound or good or efficient condition; in a state of good repair; — well-appointed (an army); — full; abundant, ample.
part. Commanded, ordered, enjoined; appointed, constituted, established, fixed, defined, determined; — s.m. Usage, established custom

ety: for A. معمورة maʻmūrat, fem. of maʻmūr
def: part. adj. = maʻmūr, q.v.; — s.m. An inhabited, or a well-peopled, place; — a cultivated spot, or a well-cultivated, or delightful, spot.
s.f. Population; populousness; cultivation; a flourishing condition; happiness; — fulness (as of a treasury, &c.); efficient condition, efficiency (of an army); — a state of good repair.
